design_philosophy.core_loop: Clear all enemies on a level, locate exit (staircase/elevator), proceed to next level. Player can ignore enemies and rush to exit, but sacrifices progression rewards (passwords).
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Survival through hostile environments filled with aliens and infected humans; time pressure from core breach countdown (few hours to prevent starship destruction); character permadeath creating squad attrition.
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Elite commando strike team member infiltrating enemy-controlled spacecraft to eliminate extraterrestrial threat and infected colonists before catastrophic explosion.
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Permadeath squad system where each of five characters can be permanently lost; player agency in mission approach (can ignore enemies for speed) but consequences affect progression access (passwords only earned through thorough clearing); emphasizes methodical clearance over rushing, creating tension between speed and completion.

difficulty.lives_system: Five selectable characters functioning as lives. When killed, character marked 'deceased' and no longer playable. Game over when all five are dead.
difficulty.checkpointing: Passwords awarded upon completing entire areas (three major areas total) only if all enemies killed. Players can reach exits without clearing enemies but forfeit password progression.
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: High agency: player can attempt levels with minimal combat engagement by rushing to exits, but this prevents password acquisition needed for progression. Risk/reward dynamic between speed and thoroughness.

progression.unlock_mechanism: Area-based progression requiring movement through 40 levels across three locations: Europa-1 (space warship), abandoned merchant freighter, Planet Defense Corps central command and sub-basement. Passwords grant access to later areas when earned through complete area clearance.
replayability_hooks: Five distinct playable characters with permadeath mechanic creates different plathrough outcomes
Two-player cooperative multiplayer mode via link cable
Optional thorough enemy clearing vs. speedrun approach creates multiple strategies
Large labyrinthine level design encourages exploration

world_building_techniques: Plot briefing establishing context (Europa-1 attacked, alien invasion, transformed humans, nuclear meltdown)
Environmental storytelling through level design: space warship interiors, freighter, military fortress, underground sub-basements
Character selection system (five-person elite squad) reinforces military narrative
Enemy types (aliens and infected humans) communicate story through gameplay

faction_indicators: Zero Tolerance (elite strike squad of five commandos)
danger_signals: Nuclear core breach countdown creating existential threat
Labyrinthine fortified military complex suggesting hostile occupation
environmental_storytelling: Space warship with fire damage and casualties
Abandoned merchant freighter suggests commercial colony disruption
Planet Defense Corps central command building (heavily fortified)
Sub-basement levels suggesting underground defense or research infrastructure
